name: bluez-hcidump
title: bluez-hcidump
description:
Analyses Bluetooth HCI packets
The hcidump utility allows the monitoring of Bluetooth activity.
It provides a disassembly of the Bluetooth traffic and can display
packets from higher level protocols such as RFCOMM, SDP and BNEP.
.
This was the software that is independent as bluez-hcidump, but this has been
integrated into BlueZ from BlueZ 5.0.
.
BlueZ is the official Linux Bluetooth protocol stack. It is an Open Source
project distributed under GNU General Public License (GPL).

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use hcidump because Analyses Bluetooth HCI packets.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use hcidump as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hcidump -h` as documented upstream for hcidump.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `hcidump [OPTIONS ...] [FILTERS]` as documented upstream for hcidump.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair hcidump with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `hcidump --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install bluez. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `hcidump -h`. Further invocations from upstream: `hcidump [OPTIONS ...] [FILTERS]`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Analyses Bluetooth HCI packets.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Analyses Bluetooth HCI packets.
